title: Split Salesforce Object Name and Label into Separate Fields
description:
Currently the Salesforce Object Label and API Name are concatenated as 'Label (API_Name)' for display. While this format works well for the end user, the underlying data model needs to store these as two separate fields.

### Rationale
- **API integrations** — Downstream automations and API integrations require the Salesforce Object API Name as a standalone value.
- **Display flexibility** — The Label is used for user-facing display while the API Name is used for programmatic lookups.
- **Data integrity** — The Object API Name never changes in Salesforce, but administrators can update the Label. Using the API Name for lookups and joins prevents breakage.

### Requirements
- Store Object Name (API name) and Object Label as separate columns in the SQL database.
- Concatenate them for display purposes only.
- Both fields should be loaded into PHP code as distinct variables.
- Neither field should be editable by the end user (they come directly from Salesforce).
acceptance_criteria: Salesforce Object API Name and Label are stored as separate columns in the SQL database
Display to end users still shows the concatenated 'Label (API_Name)' format
PHP code exposes both fields as distinct variables
Object API Name and Label fields are not editable by end users
Lookup and join operations use the API Name, not the Label
middleware_changes: PHP logic updated to handle separate Object Name and Label fields, with concatenation applied at the display layer only.
data_model_changes: Split the single concatenated field into two separate columns: one for the Salesforce Object API Name and one for the Object Label. API Name is used for all programmatic lookups and joins.
implementation_notes: Critical design constraint: never use the Object Label for lookup or join operations since Salesforce admins can change labels. The API Name is immutable and should be the primary key for integrations.
